text.title: Experiment Verdict Template Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that kill zombie experiments and convert every test into a routed decision + captured learning.

rule.statement: Every experiment ends with a verdict from exactly five values: ship-treatment, ship-control, inconclusive-iterate, inconclusive-stop, harmful-rollback. "We'll think about it" or any sixth state is forbidden.

rationale.source: Faion experiment-ops audit; cf. Statsig + GrowthBook verdict taxonomies

rule.rationale: Five verdicts cover every honest outcome; an open-ended sixth state accumulates zombie experiments.

rule.statement: Verdicts MUST be written within 5 business days of the experiment's pre-registered close date. Experiments missing the date are auto-stopped at the platform (variant assignment paused) until a verdict is written. "Running longer for more data" requires a NEW pre-registration with explicit power re-justification.
rule.rationale: Running until accidentally significant ships false positives. Forced close + explicit re-justification is the only known cure.

rule.statement: Verdicts MUST explicitly address pre-registered secondary metrics. Treatment wins on primary but loses on ≥1 secondary becomes "inconclusive-iterate" or "harmful-rollback" by default; overriding to "ship-treatment" requires a named exec sign-off captured in the card.

rationale.source: Single-metric optimization disaster archive (cf. Microsoft Bing experiments)

rule.rationale: Primary-only verdicts ship treatments that win on conversion but tank retention / refunds / NPS.

rule.statement: Each verdict MUST record one `learning` line: a generalizable claim with `confidence` tag from {weak, moderate, strong}. The learning is added to the learnings database. Verdicts without a learning are bounced back to the owner.
rule.rationale: Without captured learnings, experiments are a Brownian process — same hypotheses re-tested every 2 quarters.

rule.statement: Each verdict MUST route the next action to a named owner with a target date: ship-treatment → release owner; harmful-rollback → on-call; inconclusive-iterate → experiment owner with next pre-registration; inconclusive-stop → backlog owner who removes related items; ship-control → backlog owner. Cards without a routed action are not closed.
rule.rationale: "Verdict written" without a routed action is the politest form of zombie: closed but nothing happens.

example.code:
## EXP-2026-Q2-031 — Pricing-page hero variant B
- hypothesis: shortening the hero copy increases primary CTA clicks for cold traffic - arms: control (long copy) / B (short copy) - primary: CTA CTR — B +6.2% (95% CI [+2.1%, +10.3%], p=0.004) - secondary: signup conversion — B −1.8% (CI [−4.0%, +0.4%], NS); 7-day retention — flat - verdict: ship-treatment with monitoring (CTA win clean, signup non-significant) - learning: shorter hero copy on cold pricing traffic — moderate confidence; revisit when bottom-of-funnel data accrues. - routed: release owner @marina; release ticket REL-1402, target 2026-05-23. Monitoring alert on signup CR.
EXP-2026-Q2-031: B looks better, let's keep it running another week to confirm.
example.note: No close, no verdict vocabulary, no secondary check, no learning, no routed action — classic zombie; the team will rediscover the hypothesis next quarter.
